story.iWant: refresh token ile yeni access token alabilmek
story.soThat: oturum sürekli açık kalabilsin ve access token expire olduğunda tekrar login yapmak zorunda kalmayayım
story.tasks: - Task 1: RefreshTokenDto oluştur (AC: 2) - Task 2: TokenService.refreshTokens() method implement et (AC: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 11) - Task 3: AuthController.refresh() endpoint oluştur (AC: 1, 7, 8, 9, 10) - Task 4: Unit test yaz (AC: All) - Task 5: Integration test yaz (AC: 1, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. AC-2.4.1: POST /auth/refresh endpoint oluşturulmuş ve çalışıyor 2. AC-2.4.2: RefreshTokenDto validation yapılıyor (refreshToken UUID format) 3. AC-2.4.3: Refresh token database'de validate ediliyor (exists, not expired, valid user) 4. AC-2.4.4: Valid refresh token → Yeni access token (JWT) generate ediliyor 5. AC-2.4.5: Valid refresh token → Yeni refresh token (UUID) generate ediliyor (rotation) 6. AC-2.4.6: Eski refresh token database'den siliniyor (invalidate) 7. AC-2.4.7: Response döndürülüyor: { accessToken, refreshToken (new), expiresIn } 8. AC-2.4.8: Invalid refresh token → 401 Unauthorized 9. AC-2.4.9: Expired refresh token → 401 Unauthorized 10. AC-2.4.10: Non-existent refresh token → 401 Unauthorized 11. AC-2.4.11: Token rotation atomik yapılıyor (delete old + create new transaction)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Token refresh mechanism with rotation pattern. Valid refresh token generates new access+refresh token pair, old token invalidated. Includes atomicity requirements via Prisma transaction.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Complete token refresh flow: validate refresh token, check expiration, generate new tokens (rotation), delete old token. Database transaction ensures atomicity.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Token rotation security: single-use refresh tokens, database-backed for revocation. UUID format, 7-30 day expiry, rotation on every refresh.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Endpoint: POST /auth/refresh. Request: { refreshToken: UUID }. Response: { accessToken, refreshToken (new), expiresIn }. Errors: 401 Unauthorized for invalid/expired tokens.

doc.title: Boilerplate - Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story for token refresh: refresh token ile yeni access token alabilmek. Includes token rotation, database validation, and atomicity requirements.

doc.title: Architecture - Boilerplate
doc.snippet: Hybrid token architecture: JWT access tokens (stateless, 15-60 min) + database-stored refresh tokens (revokable, 7-30 days). Token rotation for security.

doc.title: Story 2.3: Admin Login & Token Generation
doc.snippet: TokenService methods available: generateAccessToken(), generateRefreshToken(), generateTokens(). JwtService, PrismaService, ConfigService injected. parseExpiration() handles '15m', '7d' formats.

file.reason: Contains generateAccessToken(), generateRefreshToken() methods to reuse. JwtService, PrismaService, ConfigService already injected. Need to add refreshTokens() method here.
Existing auth endpoints (register, login/admin). Need to add POST /auth/refresh endpoint following same pattern (@Public() decorator, response format).
@Public() decorator to bypass JWT guard for /auth/refresh endpoint (no JWT required, refresh token used instead).
Response DTO pattern for login endpoints. Token refresh response similar but without user field: { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn }.
DTO pattern example with class-validator decorators. RefreshTokenDto follows same pattern with @IsUUID(), @IsNotEmpty().
Existing unit tests for TokenService. Need to add refreshTokens() method tests following same mocking pattern (PrismaService, JwtService, ConfigService).
E2E test pattern for auth endpoints. Token refresh E2E tests follow same structure: create user, login, use refresh token, verify response.
Database service for RefreshToken CRUD operations. Methods: refreshToken.findUnique(), refreshToken.delete(), refreshToken.create(), $transaction().

story-context.constraints: 1. **DTO Validation**: RefreshTokenDto must use class-validator decorators (@IsUUID, @IsNotEmpty) following existing pattern in LoginAdminDto 2. **Response Format**: All auth endpoints return { success: true, data: {...} } structure (hrsync-backend pattern) 3. **Error Handling**: Use UnauthorizedException for all token validation failures (401 status), generic error messages 4. **Token Rotation**: Old refresh token MUST be deleted before/during new token creation (single-use pattern) 5. **Atomicity**: Token rotation MUST use Prisma transaction to ensure delete+create is atomic 6. **No JWT Guard**: /auth/refresh endpoint uses @Public() decorator (refresh token validation, not JWT) 7. **Reuse Existing Methods**: Use TokenService.generateAccessToken() and generateRefreshToken() from Story 2.3 8. **Database Queries**: Use prisma.refreshToken.findUnique({ where: { token }, include: { user: true } }) for single-query pattern 9. **Security**: Generic error messages for all failures ("Invalid refresh token", "Refresh token expired"), no credential disclosure 10. **Testing Pattern**: Follow existing test patterns (mock PrismaService, JwtService, ConfigService in unit tests) 11. **File Location**: New DTO at src/modules/auth/dto/refresh-token.dto.ts 12. **Method Location**: New refreshTokens() method in src/modules/auth/services/token.service.ts 13. **Endpoint Location**: New POST /auth/refresh in src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts

interface.name: POST /auth/refresh
interface.signature: POST /auth/refresh - Request: { refreshToken: string (UUID) } - Response: { success: boolean, data: { accessToken: string, refreshToken: string, expiresIn: number } }

interface.name: TokenService.refreshTokens()
interface.signature: async refreshTokens(refreshToken: string): Promise<{ accessToken: string; refreshToken: string; expiresIn: number }>

interface.name: RefreshToken Entity (Prisma)
interface.signature: model RefreshToken { id: String @id @default(uuid()), userID: String, domainID: String, token: String @unique, expiresAt: DateTime, createdAt: DateTime @default(now()) }

interface.name: PrismaService RefreshToken Methods
interface.signature: refreshToken.findUnique({ where, include }), refreshToken.delete({ where }), refreshToken.create({ data }), $transaction([...])

tests.standards: Testing follows NestJS + Jest patterns established in Epic 1 and Story 2.1-2.3. Unit tests use mocked dependencies (PrismaService, JwtService, ConfigService). E2E tests use test database with real Prisma client. Test files: *.spec.ts for unit tests, *.e2e-spec.ts for integration tests. Coverage target: >80% for services, >70% for controllers. Mock pattern: jest.fn() for methods, mock return values for database queries. E2E setup: Create test user → login → use tokens → verify response → cleanup.
tests.locations: - Unit tests: src/modules/auth/services/token.service.spec.ts - E2E tests: test/auth-refresh-token.e2e-spec.ts - Test database setup: test/jest-e2e-setup.ts - Test utilities: test/test-helpers.ts (if exists)

ideas.test: E2E: POST /auth/refresh with valid token → 200 OK, returns { success: true, data: { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn } }
Unit: TokenService.refreshTokens() with valid token → validates in database, generates new tokens, returns { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn }
Unit: TokenService.refreshTokens() → old refresh token deleted from database, new token created (verify delete call)
Unit: TokenService.refreshTokens() with non-existent token → throws UnauthorizedException("Invalid refresh token")
Unit: TokenService.refreshTokens() with expired token → throws UnauthorizedException("Refresh token expired"), expired token deleted
Unit: TokenService.refreshTokens() with deleted user → throws UnauthorizedException("Invalid user")
Unit: Token rotation atomicity → use prisma.$transaction([delete, create]) to ensure atomic operation
E2E: Verify old refresh token cannot be reused after refresh (401 Unauthorized on second attempt)
E2E: New refresh token stored in database, new access token is valid JWT (decode and verify payload)
E2E: Invalid/expired/non-existent refresh tokens → 401 Unauthorized with generic error message
E2E: Concurrent refresh requests (race condition test) → first wins, second fails
E2E: RefreshTokenDto validation → invalid UUID format returns 400 Bad Request
